text.title: Sunset vs Fold Framework Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Sunset vs Fold Framework plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: The decision MUST score the candidate product on five criteria (revenue, strategic adjacency, migration cost, team morale, customer concentration); decisions without the matrix MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Sunset vs fold is high-stakes and irreversible; the matrix is the structural gate against gut-only calls.

rule.statement: Folding capability into another product MUST be ratified by the receiving product's owner in writing; unilateral folds MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Unilateral folds create resentment and shipping debt; written consent is the only durable contract.

rule.statement: Sunsets MUST publish a named migration path (alternative product, data export, refund policy) before the kill date; migration-less sunsets MUST be delayed.
rule.rationale: Sunsets without migration paths trigger refund waves and reputational damage; the migration path is the structural floor.

rule.statement: Customer notice MUST be ≥90 days before the sunset date; shorter notice requires legal sign-off.
rule.rationale: Sub-90-day notice causes contractual breaches on annual contracts; 90 days is the safe default.

rule.statement: The decision record MUST be versioned and signed by the founder + at least one VP; verbal decisions MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Verbal decisions on sunsets get re-litigated quarterly; the signed record is the closure mechanism.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
